name: Shirley Scott
abstract: Shirley Scott (March 14, 1934 – March 10, 2002) was an American jazz organist. Her music was noted for its mixture of bebop, blues, and gospel elements.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
WLIB: 12th Annual Festival of Music and Drama
Newport Jazz Festival: Jazz on the Hammond

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Shirley Scott in the context of a club room where she played, but does not describe a personal or professional relationship with her.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Evans
about_quotes.evidence: people like Bobby Watson and Ralph Peterson, Ralph Bowen and all these, Shirley Scott, Trudy Pitts, just people that I was around in the neighborhood.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Evans interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 22, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Doug Carn
about_quotes.evidence: My uncle was good friends with Shirley and Stan and you know Trudy Pitts, Jimmy McGriff, I followed every one of them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doug Carn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Yusef Lateef
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...my father had recordings by yourself, and Duke Ellington, and, and, I mean, everybody, Shirley Scott.
about_quotes.doc_title: Yusef Lateef — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy McRae
about_quotes.evidence: Shirley Scott and Stanley did Broadway. Then he did Broadway again with his own group -- somebody else on piano

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: There was a whole bunch of acts, Gloria Lynne, Sonny Rollins, all these – Shirley Scott, Stanley Turrentine...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
